title: Inventory Sync Background Worker

summary.overview: This PR introduces a background worker for synchronizing inventory data between the warehouse management system and the product catalog. The implementation uses a polling-based approach with configurable intervals and batch processing. Overall the architecture is sound, but error handling around failed sync batches needs attention before production deployment.

riskAssessment.summary: The worker processes inventory updates in batches but lacks a dead-letter queue for failed messages, which could result in silent data loss during upstream service outages.
items: No dead-letter queue configured for failed sync messages
Batch size of 500 records may cause memory pressure under peak load
Missing circuit breaker pattern for external warehouse API calls
Database migration adds a new table but lacks rollback script

strengths: Clean separation between queue producer, worker logic, and reconciliation service follows single-responsibility principle
Comprehensive test coverage at 89% with well-structured integration tests for the sync pipeline
Configurable polling interval and batch size allow tuning without code changes
Idempotent sync operations prevent duplicate inventory updates on retry

issues.message: Failed sync batches are logged but silently discarded without routing to a dead-letter queue, which means inventory discrepancies will go undetected during partial outages.
issues.recommendation: Implement a dead-letter queue using the existing RabbitMQ infrastructure and configure alerts when the DLQ depth exceeds a threshold.

issues.message: The reconciliation service makes sequential HTTP calls to the warehouse API for each SKU in the batch instead of using the bulk endpoint.
issues.recommendation: Refactor to use the /api/v2/warehouse/bulk-lookup endpoint to reduce network round trips and improve throughput by an estimated 10x.

issues.message: Default polling interval is set to 5 seconds which may be too aggressive for non-production environments and could trigger rate limiting.
issues.recommendation: Set the default polling interval to 30 seconds and allow environment-specific overrides via the existing configuration hierarchy.

issues.message: Queue connection string is hardcoded as a fallback when the environment variable is missing, exposing an internal hostname.
issues.recommendation: Remove the hardcoded fallback and throw a descriptive startup error if QUEUE_CONNECTION_STRING is not set.

recommendations: Add a dead-letter queue with monitoring and alerting for failed sync messages before merging to production
Implement a circuit breaker pattern around the warehouse API client to gracefully handle upstream outages
Add a database migration rollback script to the migrations directory following the existing rollback naming convention
Consider adding a health check endpoint that reports the last successful sync timestamp for operational monitoring
Document the sync worker configuration options in the service README for the on-call team
